stimulus: An exit survey conducted after the 1992 mayoral debate in Riverdale indicated that challenger Mara Velasquez had stumbled: only 18 percent of attendees who listened to the audio broadcast rated her the stronger speaker. ______blank the debate was widely reported afterward as a turning point for the photogenic challenger, who polled markedly higher than the incumbent among viewers watching the new local cable feed.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. "However" logically signals that the claim in this sentence—that the debate was reported as a turning point for Velasquez—is true despite the previous information about the audio-broadcast survey suggesting she had stumbled.

Choice B is incorrect because "therefore" illogically signals that the claim is a result of the audio-broadcast survey, when it actually runs against what that survey suggested. Choice C is incorrect because "likewise" illogically signals that this claim is similar to the previous information, when it in fact contrasts with the impression created by that survey. Choice D is incorrect because "in other words" illogically signals that the claim is a paraphrase of the previous information, when it actually presents a contrary outcome.
